scope.summary: Rust qualified/import-backed block_on calls and proof-backed runtime receiver chains now report shared future scheduling and result-channel obligations.

capability_alignment.capability: future drive scheduling attribution for sync/async bridges
not_a_feature_list: No exact admission path was opened for Rust block_on or async/sync equivalence.
No new public semantic-pack API was added.
Selector-only .block_on calls stay closed unless the receiver is rooted in proof-backed tokio runtime construction, Handle::current evidence, or an allowed root-preserving constructor chain.
self.rt.block_on and variable-typed runtime receivers remain closed until field/type identity evidence exists.

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The tokio_test root must not be project-local, and imported bindings must be unshadowed and scoped to the occurrence.
The tokio root must not be project-local, and imported Handle evidence must be unshadowed and scoped to the occurrence.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: proof-backed inline Runtime::new()/Builder::new_*() receiver chains ending in .block_on(...)
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The inline receiver chain must contain qualified or imported tokio::runtime Runtime/Builder construction proof.

assertions: Imported tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on reports future-drive and future-settled obligations.
Qualified tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on reports future-drive and future-settled obligations.
Imported tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on reports future-drive and future-settled obligations.
Imported tokio_test::block_on reports future-drive and future-settled obligations.
An arbitrary receiver.block_on call does not receive future-drive attribution.
A project-local tokio root blocks qualified tokio::runtime::Handle attribution.
A local Handle parameter blocks imported Handle attribution.
A local block_on function blocks imported tokio_test::block_on attribution.
The recall-loss report rolls Rust block_on under scheduling-boundary/future-drive-scheduling-contract-missing.
The report keeps task spawn and aggregate obligations unchanged.

current_crates_gate.note: The local crates surface has no qualified/import-backed Rust block_on bridge rows; real-corpus Tokio spot checks exercise the new attribution.

summary.primary_reason_note: The block_on(async { sleep_until(...).await }) unit also carries async-await evidence, which remains the primary scheduling subreason by rule order.

closed_surfaces.reason: The current reporter does not infer struct-field or local-variable tokio runtime types.
Selector spelling alone is not runtime identity proof.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between block_on, await, and synchronous payload values
closed_surfaces.reason: Future drive scheduling, wake/poll behavior, cancellation/liveness, and result-channel semantics remain missing evidence.

next_exact_work_policy.summary: Rust future-drive bridges are now attributable, but exact async/sync recovery still needs poll/wake scheduling, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, and receiver/type identity contracts.
